notes: Eval 2 (vague 'fix the auth') passes 100% in both configurations — task is so obviously underspecified that even without the skill, the model correctly asks for clarification. This eval doesn't differentiate skill value.
The skill's primary value shows in Evals 1 and 3: it enforces structured knowns/unknowns/assumptions separation and a formal readiness review — both missing from baselines.

Critical behavioral difference not captured by assertions: baselines for Evals 1 and 3 jumped into deep technical analysis, architecture decisions, and task breakdowns with time estimates. The skill successfully constrained the agent to PREPARATION ONLY — no premature analysis. This is arguably the skill's most important function.
Skill adds ~55s average execution time and ~2k tokens, but improves pass rate by 24%. The time cost is primarily the readiness critique step.

The readiness critic subagent could not be spawned as a real subagent in the test runs (Agent tool unavailable in nested context) — critique was done inline. A real subagent would provide better isolation and objectivity.
